publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 02:23:54 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Business records and curatorial files of Diego Cortez containing documentation of his work with galleries and artists, including information on artists and writers such as Duncan Smith, Henry Geld, Willam Burroughs, Nan Golden, Basquiat, Jimmy Desana, Julian Schnabel.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born James Allen Curtis in Geneva, Illinois in 1946, Curtis moved to New York City in 1973 and assumed the name Diego Cortez. He became well-known as an artist, club manager, promoter, and independent curator, co-founding the influential NYC nightspot The Mudd Club, which offered an eclectic mix of art, music and cultural events that combined both modernist and punk aesthetics. The Mudd Club operated at 77 White Street in downtown Manhattan between 1978 and 1983. In 1981, Cortez curated the groundbreaking exhibition "New York, No Wave" at a defunct elementary school in Long Island City, Queens called P.S. 1. The exhibition featured the work of David Byrne, Keith Haring, Robert Mapplethorpe, Jean-Michel Basquiat, and Kenny Scharf, among many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed poster of poem by Paul Duffy and illustrated by Allen Duffy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spring issue includes pictorial feature on Cortez party. Cortez listed as contributing editor for Fall issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note from George H.W. Bush.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clipping from high school (circa 1964).

did.unittitle: Loans of art from Diego Cortez Collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folders chiefly contain correspondence, clippings, photographs, and notes on bands and musicians, including Blonde Redhead and Arto Lindsay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains rubber necklace designed by Maripol for official merchandise for Madonna's 1985 Virgin Tour.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcards, letters, notes, and miscellaneous printed materials, chieefly from the 1970s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Cortez's cub scout membership card and confirmation certificate.

did.unittitle: Naked Lunch: a screenplay by David Cronenberg based on the novel by William S. Burroughs -- fifth draft
The North Wind's Gift, from medieval folktale adpated by Italo Calvino, screenplay by Michael Almereya

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopied card from writer Victor Bockris and thank you note from the family of Roy Lichtenstein upon his death in 1997.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains original photos of President Taft.

did.unittitle: Sevilla casa c/ Santa Ana no. 19
Duncan Smith -- Col. and Mil. (Collins and Milazzo) letter
Malcolm X -- Grassroots speech, Detroit, Michigan
Diego-- X-mas cards, press pass, birth cert
Diego-- X-mas cards, press pass, birth cert. X-mas cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series contains records of artists Cortez worked with and exhibitions he either curated or contributed to, including those of Jean Michel Basquiat, Gilbert and George, and William S. Burroughs. In addition to correspondence and printed media (such as exhibition catalogues and postcards), the sub-series is rich with visual records, including photographs, slides, and transparencies. Of note is original artwork by Keith Haring and mementoes from Andy Warhol's funeral.

did.unittitle: Art Against AIDS at the 100th Venice Biennale, corporate sponsorship proposal

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See box 6, folder 12 for drafts of Cortez's article, "Francesco Clemente's Fresco della Piscina," and box 6, folder 24 for undated dinner speech by Cortez.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See box 6, folder 12 for drafts of Cortez's article, "Francesco Clemente's Fresco della Piscina," and box 6, folder 24 for undated dinner speech by Cortez.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See Box 6, folder 13 for Cortez's interview with Cucchi for Interview magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of limited edition artist book.

did.unittitle: Bruce Davenport, Jr. -- Good Stuff to Look At
De Sana -- CCD Galerie exh./ Düsseldorf
Sam Doyle exh. -- Pat Hearn Gal.
Gyuto butter sculpture exhibit -- American Museum of Nat'l Hist. opening ceremony

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also box 7, folder 9 and 10 for records on Haring's mural collaboration with Chicago Public School students.

did.unittitle: Images for an exhibition at Duke University. Diego Cortez

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials for "William S. Burroughs & Philip Taaffe: Drawing Dialogue", Pat Hearn Gallery, NYC (catalogue), 1987. Folder also contains contact prints of Taaffe and Burroughs.

did.unittitle: Taaffe -- Lower East Side Printshop, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folders include Warhol's funeral mass cards, funeral poster, and a card from his cousin, Christine Soley, containing a photograph of him.

did.unittitle: Series III. Writings, lectures, and speeches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contain notes and drafts for Private Elvis, edited by Cortez. Text chiefly by Duncan Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contain notes and drafts for Private Elvis, edited by Cortez. Text chiefly by Duncan Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edited and signed by Thurston Moore.

did.unittitle: The Most Beautiful Day of My Life: a lecture by Bernard Faucon; introduction by Diego Cortez

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster for event held at Institute of the Arts and Humanities, UNC-Chapel Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and photographs related to Keith Haring and his mural for Chicago Public Schools.

did.unittitle: Diego Cortez -- July '94 -- Capri and Aug. '94
D. Cortez. Stuzzi Cadenti and related
Losing Fear, Fearing Fear, Using Fear. Capri-- new short fiction w/ notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series contains correspondence and invoices between Cortez and art collectors. Included are photographs, slides, and transparencies of artworks.

did.unittitle: GF -- Aubry, Braun, etc. (19th c. foto)
GF (George Fineberg), Boston: Museum of Fine Arts
Series V. Galleries, museums, and dealers
Anthony D'Offay Gallery -- Gilbert and George
John Hope Franklin Center at Duke
Property from the Collection of Henry Geldzahler -- Christie's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various films and videos Cortez made as an artist.

did.unittitle: Boston 'Political' Sampler, a/ medical, b/ rock, c/political
Collaborative Projects Group. Medical Sampler. Tape A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced at Albert Einstein Medical Center/Bronx, N. York (October 1976) with the cooperation of Dr. Kenneth Shulman.
